FullText: » The testimony I have is greater than that of John . The works I do are the works the Father gave me to accomplish . They testify that the Father has sent me .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'the Father' corresponds to the semantic agent (doer/initiator) of the target verb 'has sent', and the grammatical object 'me' corresponds to the semantic patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: » The Father who sent me testifies about me . You have not heard his voice nor seen him at any time .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'You' corresponds to the semantic agent (the one doing the hearing), not the patient, and there is no 'been' auxiliary, so it is not passive.

FullText: » The Father who sent me testifies about me . You have not heard his voice nor seen him at any time .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'You' corresponds to the semantic agent of the verb 'seen', not the patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: » You do not have his word dwelling in you . This is because you do not believe in the person he has sent .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'he' corresponds to the semantic agent of the verb 'sent', and the grammatical object 'person' corresponds to the semantic patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: » Do not think that I will accuse you before the Father . Moses on whom you have set your hope is a plaintiff against you .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'you' corresponds to the semantic agent (the one who sets the hope), and the grammatical object 'hope' corresponds to the semantic patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: They gathered twelve baskets of leftovers after everyone had eaten .
explanation: The target 'had eaten' is in the active voice, where the subject 'everyone' is the agent and there is no 'been' auxiliary.

FullText: They boarded a boat to travel over the sea to Capernaum . It was dark and Jesus had not yet come to them .
explanation: The target 'had not yet come' does not have the form auxiliary 'have' + 'been' + past participle of a transitive verb, and the subject 'Jesus' is the agent, not the patient.

FullText: » Do not work for food that perishes but for food that offers everlasting life . The Son of man will give you this food . The Father , even God , has set his seal upon him . «
explanation: The grammatical subject 'The Father' corresponds to the semantic agent of the verb 'set', and the grammatical object 'his seal' corresponds to the semantic patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: Jesus answered : » This is the work of God , that you have an active faith in the one whom he has sent . «
explanation: The grammatical subject 'he' corresponds to the semantic agent of the verb 'sent', not the patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: » I told you . You have seen me and yet do not believe .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'You' corresponds to the semantic agent of the verb 'see', not the patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: » This is the will of him that sent me that I should not lose any of those he has given me , but I should raise them up at the last day .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'he' corresponds to the semantic agent (giver) of the verb 'has given', and the grammatical object 'me' corresponds to the semantic patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: » It is written in the prophets : Jehovah shall teach them all . Every one who hears the Father and has learned comes to me .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'one' corresponds to the semantic agent (doer/initiator) of the target verb 'has learned', and the grammatical object is missing, indicating an active voice.

FullText: » No one has seen the Father . Only he who is from God , He has seen the Father .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'no one' corresponds to the semantic agent of the verb 'seen', not the patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: » No one has seen the Father . Only he who is from God , He has seen the Father .

FullText: » You go to this feast . I will not go to this feast for my time has not yet come . «
explanation: The complement of 'been' is a non-verbal predicate (NP/AP/PP) rather than a verbal past participle functioning as a passive; in this case 'come' is not a passive participle.

FullText: The Jews wondered saying : » How does this man know letters ? He has had no formal education . «
explanation: The complement of 'been' is a non-verbal predicate (NP/AP/PP) rather than a verbal past participle functioning as a passive; in this case 'been' is the lexical copula, not a passive auxiliary.

FullText: They looked for a way to capture him . But no man laid his hand on him . This is because his hour had not yet arrived .
explanation: The target 'had not yet arrived' does not have the form auxiliary 'have' + 'been' + past participle of a transitive verb, and the subject 'his hour' is the agent.

FullText: Many in the crowd believed in him . They asked : » When the Christ comes will he do more signs than those this man has done ? «
explanation: The grammatical subject 'this man' corresponds to the semantic agent of the verb 'done', not the patient, and the sentence does not contain the passive auxiliary 'been'.

FullText: » No man has ever spoken the way he speaks , « they replied .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'man' corresponds to the semantic agent (doer/initiator) of the target verb, and the grammatical object 'the way he speaks' corresponds to the semantic patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: » Does our law convict a man without first hearing from him to find out what he has done ? «
explanation: The grammatical subject 'he' corresponds to the semantic agent of the verb 'done', not the patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: He spoke these words at the treasury as he taught in the temple . No one tried to arrest him because his hour had not yet arrived .
explanation: The target 'has not yet arrived' does not have the form auxiliary 'have' + 'been' + past participle of a transitive verb, and the subject is the agent, not the patient.

FullText: » Who are you , « they asked . Jesus replied : » What have I been telling you from the beginning ?
explanation: The target 'have been telling' is in the active progressive voice, not the passive voice, because it does not have the form 'have been + past participle' and the subject 'I' is the agent.

FullText: Jesus said : » When you have lifted up impaled the Son of man you will know that I am that person . I do nothing by myself ! I speak what the Father taught me !
explanation: The target 'have lifted up' is in the active voice, not the passive voice, because the subject 'you' is the agent performing the action of lifting.

FullText: » He sent me and is with me . He has not left me alone . I always do the things that are pleasing to him ! «
explanation: The grammatical subject 'He' corresponds to the semantic agent of the verb 'left', not the patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: They answered him : » We are Abraham’s seed , and have never been in bondage to any man . How can you say we will be made free ? «
explanation: The complement of 'been' is a prepositional phrase ('in bondage') rather than a verbal past participle functioning as a passive, so 'been' is the lexical copula, not a passive auxiliary.

FullText: » I speak the things that I have seen and heard when I was with my Father . You also do the things that you heard from your father . «
explanation: The grammatical subject 'I' corresponds to the semantic agent (doer/initiator) of the target verb 'have seen', and the grammatical object 'the things' corresponds to the semantic patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: » I speak the things that I have seen and heard when I was with my Father . You also do the things that you heard from your father . «
explanation: The grammatical subject 'I' corresponds to the semantic agent (doer/initiator) of the target verb, and the grammatical object 'the things' corresponds to the semantic patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: Jesus said : » If God were your Father you would love me . I came from God ! I have not come from myself . He sent me !
explanation: The target 'have not come' is not in passive voice because it does not have the form 'have been + past participle' and the subject 'I' is the agent.

FullText: The Jews replied : » You are not yet fifty years old . Have you seen Abraham ? «
explanation: The grammatical subject 'you' corresponds to the semantic agent (the one doing the seeing), and the grammatical object 'Abraham' corresponds to the semantic patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: The Jews did not believe that he had been blind and then received his sight . So they called his parents .
explanation: The complement of 'been' is a non-verbal predicate (adjective 'blind') rather than a verbal past participle functioning as a passive; in this case 'been' is the lexical copula, not a passive auxiliary.

FullText: The Jews did not believe that he had been blind and then received his sight . So they called his parents .
explanation: The target does not have the form auxiliary 'have' + 'been' + past participle of a transitive verb, and the grammatical subject corresponds to the semantic agent of the target verb.

FullText: The parents said these things because they feared the Jews . The Jews had agreed that if any man should confess him to be Christ , he should be put out of the synagogue .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'The Jews' corresponds to the semantic agent of the verb 'agreed', not the patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: » No one takes it away from me , but I lay it down voluntarily . I have authority jurisdiction right to lay it down , and I have authority to take it again . I have received this commandment from my Father . «
explanation: The grammatical subject 'I' corresponds to the semantic agent (receiver) of the verb 'have received', not the patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: » My Father has given them to me . He is greater than all and no one is able to snatch them out of the Father’s hand .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'My Father' corresponds to the semantic agent of the verb 'give', not the patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: Jesus replied to them : » I have shown you many good works from the Father . For which of those works do you stone me ? «
explanation: The grammatical subject 'I' corresponds to the semantic agent (doer/initiator) of the target verb, and the grammatical object 'you' corresponds to the semantic patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: Then he told them : » Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep . I go to wake him out of sleep . «
explanation: The target 'has fallen asleep' is not passive because 'fallen' is not the past participle of a transitive verb used in a passive construction; it's part of the intransitive verb 'fall asleep'.

FullText: The disciples said , » Lord , if he has fallen asleep , he would recover . «
explanation: The target 'has fallen asleep' is not passive because 'fallen' is not the past participle of a transitive verb used in a passive construction; it is part of an intransitive verb phrase indicating a state.

FullText: When Jesus arrived he found Lazarus had been in the grave four days .
explanation: The complement of 'been' is a prepositional phrase ('in the grave') rather than a verbal past participle functioning as a passive, so 'been' is the lexical copula, not a passive auxiliary.

FullText: Martha said to Jesus : » Lord if you had been here my brother would not have died .
explanation: The complement of 'been' is an adverb ('here') rather than a verbal past participle functioning as a passive, so 'been' is the lexical copula, not a passive auxiliary.

FullText: Martha said to Jesus : » Lord if you had been here my brother would not have died .
explanation: The target 'would not have died' is not in passive voice because it does not have the form 'have been + past participle' and the subject 'brother' is the agent.

FullText: When Mary saw Jesus she fell down at his feet . She said : » Lord ; if you had been here my brother would not have died . «
explanation: The complement of 'been' is an adverb ('here') rather than a verbal past participle functioning as a passive, so 'been' is the lexical copula, not a passive auxiliary.

FullText: When Mary saw Jesus she fell down at his feet . She said : » Lord ; if you had been here my brother would not have died . «
explanation: The grammatical subject 'my brother' corresponds to the semantic agent (the one performing the action of dying), not the patient, and there is no passive morphology present.

FullText: He asked : » Where have you laid him ? « They answered : » Come and see Lord . «
explanation: The grammatical subject 'you' corresponds to the semantic agent (doer/initiator) of the target verb, and the grammatical object 'him' corresponds to the semantic patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: Some of them said : » This man opened the eyes of the blind . Could he have prevented this man from dying ? «
explanation: The target 'have prevented' is in the active voice, not the passive voice, because the subject 'he' is the agent of the verb 'prevented'.

FullText: Jesus said : » Take away the stone . « Martha , the sister of the dead man said : » Lord by now the body has decayed for he has been dead four days . «
explanation: The target 'has decayed' does not have the form auxiliary 'have' + 'been' + past participle of a transitive verb, and the subject 'body' is the agent, not the patient.

FullText: Jesus said : » Take away the stone . « Martha , the sister of the dead man said : » Lord by now the body has decayed for he has been dead four days . «

FullText: Some of them went to the Pharisees and told them the things that Jesus had done .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'Jesus' corresponds to the semantic agent (doer/initiator) of the target verb 'had done', and the grammatical object 'the things' corresponds to the semantic patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had given orders that , if any man knew where he was , he should report it that they might seize him .
explanation: The grammatical subject 'the chief priests and the Pharisees' corresponds to the semantic agent of the verb 'had given', not the semantic patient, indicating an active voice construction.

FullText: His disciples did not understand these things at first . However when Jesus was glorified they remembered the things that were written about him . They also remembered the things that had been done to him .
explanation: The gram 'had been done' has the form auxiliary 'have' + 'been' + past participle of a transitive verb, and the grammatical subject corresponds to the semantic patient.
